Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Shield card stands for those that defend us, and those who in turn defend them. The Shield is defence, armour, the thin blue line and a standing army. It can mean that something needs defending, that an attack is coming, and serves as a warning.

Significator.Plot: The Shield Plot seeks to defend a position. The Plot may favour a particular Plot Resolution, and will seek to actively defend that position against the Characters. Such Plots can be frustrating, or exciting, depending upon the situation, and the Players actions and resolve. The Shield Plot usually protects the status-quo of the world, always seeking to return the normal balance to the local universe, but it can be overcome and great change brought about through that paradigm shift.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked by the Shield, they will act as the Guardians of the status-quo during the Plot. Trying to stop the Completion from occurring at all if possible, prolonging the Loom as long as they can, or favouring Rejection, Revelation, Reversion, and Revocation endings in that order.
Significator.Character:
The Shield Significator indicates a Character who acts to protect society, as well as individuals.

They Gain Yin when they defend the status-quo, or a weaker Character.

Gain Yang when they attack an enemy of the state, criminal or traitor to their society.

They Gain Chi when they protect any group or pact which they are part of.

They gain +1 Success Levels to all active defences.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A steadfast guardian and protector, dedicated to the safety of others and the preservation of order. They act as a bulwark against those who would do harm, finding their greatest strength in the defense of their community and the upholding of the law.
Significator.Scene: The Shield Scene is usually used as a Weft, to protect or shield the Character from harm. It can be used as a Defence Ordeal, forcing the Character to defend themselves against some attack or accident. Yarn-Tellers usually play this card as an Aegis defence, protecting the target from all damage for the duration of the Scene, although they can try the target's defences too.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Incarna, this is often a threat to life and limb, or an offer for healing a significant Wound, training or improvement of the Incarna Facet.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Conflict Hooks the Character with a powerful, Tone setting Scene. The Tone should reflect the Dominant side of the Conflict, usually an under represented Facet on that side of the Conflict. This tone setting serves as a warning of what to expect later.
Yarn.Gain: A Defence Descendant (armour, gauntlets, shield, anti-virus software, insurance, etc)
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: A robust means of protection—a set of sturdy armor, a reliable shield, or a sophisticated security system—that ensures safety against future threats.
Yarn.Fray_Description: This is total war, and during total war you do not go into battle without putting on armour, strapping on a shield, and raising your deflector fields. This is an Extreme Stakes Opposed Ordeal, where the PCs usually defend a position, vehicle or person that's under attack.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray is set at least partially in a fortress, police station, dungeon, maximum security prison, airbase, or similarly well-defended place. During the Redoubt Snag every Stage has at least one Obstacle and double the number normally expected in this sort of Ordeal. Stakes will be raised one higher (and Black Twos Wild), and add in as many Vex and Chorus Extras as you think the PCs can possibly handle, and then a handful of Cast Extras on top, that will be scattered throughout the Redoubt.

NPC.Description: The Parent is an archetype as common as the Mother, or the Father, they are seen as protectors, but family situations often cause arguments.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs will be protected, usually by an Ally and/or Location from a Warp (The Ends, The Fray & The Snag) that does affect the other side(s) of the Conflict.
Yarn.History_Description: The Shield indicates that before the current situation there was a suitable defensive force that defended everyone from this precise problem, what happened to it is probably the source of the current situation.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Shield indicates that the current situation will require you to defend yourself, often from a more powerful opponent, although you usually have an advantage in defences.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Shield indicates that the solution to the current situation is to guard and keep watch, the enemy will reveal themselves eventually, and you must be ready.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, all the sides and anything in between. It is normal when providing information to always reveal information about the Dominant Side as well as any others.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information is about the defences of the Embodiment. Do they always have an anti-stab vest, or security detail, is the location a veritable fortress, or does the computer have an AI Firewall?
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is presented by or in a device of sort sort. This might be the shield of a knight baring his coat of arms, or its modern equivalent the unit patch, or the device may be a scanner, radio, or often now a phone that provides the information.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about the privileges that the Embodiment enjoys. Whether they are from a rich family, inherited land, had a good education, were not rejected by society despite their crimes, or whether they are trusted with a hall pass because of their status as head of some club or another. Privileges were originally private laws that effectively protect an individual or select group, often granting then immunity or exemption from another law.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Ambient details are atmospheric touches and subtle clues about the Embodiment. They are the hints of lilly scent on the air near the vampire, the shadows deepening as the demon draws breath, or the ultra-high pitched electronic whine that only children and animals can hear, that the otherwise perfect android makes.

Obstacle.Description: The way ahead is blocked by some shield, walls, fences, gates, doors, guards, or perhaps automated laser turrets. Twice the normal Difficulty to pass or disable.

Tarot.Crossed: Target may disregard current RT, GRT, Pool Size and take a Full-Action.
Tarot.Normal: Target is immediately affected by GRT of 2

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The way ahead is flooded. It might be an ocean, sea, lake, a river, a pond, or a storm drain, but there is water ahead.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Roaring engines, skipping hulls and Rooster-tail wakes, or whistling wind, humming lines and the slap of waves on wood, either way, they'll get away, unless you catch or kill them!
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces engulf and surround the Ebbing side. The Ebbing enemy must break out of this box, surrender or be destroyed.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
Either a solitary Monster or small team (2-3) of Chorus Extras guard the way ahead.
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Stress.Description: Stress can interfere with your life, making it more complicated in every way. Self-sabotage can play a part too, stress acts against our better judgement and instincts at times, interfering in what we do.
Stress.Rules: All Actions in the suited Facets (Burden, Dominion, Fury, Rook, Sin, or Wyrd) add a Difficulty equal to the Character’s current Stress in Tao Sway. If a penalised Action Succeeds it will reduce the Character’s Stress by 1.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can occasionally be helpful, just as surgery is a case of trauma excising some weakness of the body, so too can trauma remove a perceived weakness of the mind. Characters who are used to certain forms of abuse or trauma will develop coping and defence mechanisms, some of which are more effective than others.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma teaches the Character a new Defence Mechanism that they may employ in future. [t13ne type="table" array="psychologicalDefences" /]. When the Character uses this preferred psychological Defence mechanism they will Relieve 1 Stress automatically.

Age.Description: An Age of Protection is occurring. During this age the society acts to create a safe environment for itself, its members and its allies. Protective societies often include social outreach, charities for the poor and disadvantaged (at least members, sometimes allies), as well as socialised, and sometimes professional, military and health care (usually).
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of security and collective care, where society prioritizes the protection and well-being of its members. It is an era of fortified defenses and social outreach, characterized by a shared commitment to building a safe and stable environment for all.
Age.Rules: During the Age of Protection the society attempts to protect itself, its members and its allies, often by building defences, obstacles, training guards or police etc.
